title: Common Mitigation Enumeration (CME) Entry
description: A structured defensive control entry in the CME taxonomy, designed to complement CVE/CWE by identifying mitigations and mapping their deterministic impact on CVSS environmental scoring.

control_name.description: Formal name of the mitigation control

description.description: Technical description of what the control does and how it mitigates risk

category.description: Sub-category within the tactic (e.g., Kernel Hardening, Network Isolation)

control_layer.description: Technology stack layer where the control resides

cvss_vector_impacts.description: One or more CVSS base metric modifications this control provides

cwe_relationships.description: CWE weakness classes this control mitigates

method.description: Human-readable description of how to verify the control is active

expected.description: Expected output or pattern indicating the control is active

verification.description: How a scanner or agent can confirm the control is active

d3fend_mapping.description: Mapping to equivalent D3FEND technique if one exists

references.description: External references (Red Hat docs, NIST, CIS benchmarks, etc.)

confidence.description: Confidence level that the CVSS attenuation is deterministic and verifiable

platforms.description: Platforms where this control is applicable (e.g., RHEL 9, Ubuntu 24.04, Kubernetes)

from.description: Original metric value (e.g., N, L, H, C)

to.description: Modified metric value when control is active

rationale.description: Technical justification for this specific attenuation
